item.title: Erdogan and Putin, the End of an Unlikely Partnership
item.description: Russia’s decline has given Turkey the freedom to pursue its interests, and Ukraine is the beneficiary.

item.title: Washington Needs to Account for Its Bad Wars
item.description: The American foreign policy class should shed its addiction to military force.

item.title: A Dark Vision of Masculinity
item.description: Why does the modern, online vision of masculinity feel so “deeply depressing”? In conversation with the journalist Helen Lewis, the Opinion columnist Ezra Klein explains how leading right-wing figures weaponize the word “virtue” to mask a lack of moral and civic obligation.

item.title: Behind Every Dad Bod Is a Healthy Dad Brain
item.description: The benefits of fatherhood are deep and long lasting.

item.title: Graham Platner and the Rise of the ‘Dirtbag’ Democrat
item.description: And what the Maine candidate reveals about politics today.

item.title: Escape From Trumpism
item.description: The best pathway out is to make Congress great again.

item.title: The States Where Life Is Better
item.description: Trust, well-being and mental health are all down in America. But some states are better to live in than others, according to a new study.

item.title: Norma Jeane’s Still Got It!
item.description: Some continue to like it hot.

item.title: When Is It Wrong to Use A.I.?
item.description: How to criticize an inevitable disruption.

item.title: Who Should Get an A at Harvard?
item.description: Readers respond to a guest essay about the university’s new 20 percent cap on A grades.

item.title: Graham Platner and the Rise of the ‘Dirtbag’ Democrat
item.description: And what the Maine candidate reveals about politics today.

item.title: Bronze Age Pervert’s ‘Cosplay’ Masculinity
item.description: “This is an ideology that is born out of fat modernity itself,” the journalist Helen Lewis argues on “The Ezra Klein Show,” where she explains why the online right’s fascination with hypermasculinity is a bored delusion made possible by the safety of modern life.

item.title: The White House’s Latest Provocation Is Way More Than Just a Troll
item.description: What’s the matter, can’t you take a joke?

item.title: Melinda French Gates: Women, We Deserve Better Than This
item.description: Too many women walk out of their doctors’ office with no diagnosis, no treatment and no plan.

item.title: The British Used to Sound Like We Did
item.description: When they left, they changed.

item.title: The New Right’s Very Old Vision of Men
item.description: The journalist Helen Lewis examines the ancient, angry gender politics of the New Right.

item.title: Trump Wants Results Against Crime in Mexico. Can Sheinbaum Deliver?
item.description: This is Claudia Sheinbaum’s worst crisis. It might also be her best chance.

item.title: Multimember Districts? Ranked Choice? This Is How to Fix Our Elections.
item.description: Americans should think big about shaking up how we vote.

item.title: The World Cup Is Starting Soon. Why Does No One Care?
item.description: It isn’t just because the tournament is being held in America under Trump.

item.title: It’s No Wonder Grads Are Booing Their Commencement Speakers
item.description: Companies are trying to automate graduates’ futures away. No wonder they’re furious.

item.title: Trump Has Become What He Feared
item.description: “Trump loses more often, on more things, than most.”

item.title: America Broke Something When It Gave Trump a Second Chance
item.description: Lincoln would know how to deal with these Republicans.

item.title: The Betrayal of Black Voters Threatens Our Democracy
item.description: The sweeping effort to dismantle Black-majority congressional districts in the South will have far-reaching consequences for all Americans, and for our democracy.

item.title: I Went to Lebanon and Found Something Strangely Hopeful
item.description: It just needs to be given the chance.

item.title: A Divide Over U.S. Support for Israel
item.description: Readers respond to Senator Chris Van Hollen’s call on the Democratic Party to push Israel to work on a two-state solution. Also: Options for mothers.

item.title: The New Right’s Very Old Vision of Men
item.description: The journalist Helen Lewis examines the ancient, angry gender politics of the New Right.

item.title: This Man Should Not Be in Charge of National Intelligence
item.description: Pulte’s one evident qualification is his eagerness to advance the president’s political revenge campaign.

item.title: Anna Paulina Luna Wants Everything Disclosed
item.description: The MAGA Representative on Epstein, J.F.K. and U.F.O.s.

item.title: The Supreme Court’s Jim Crow Logic
item.description: In Alabama, the Supreme Court just dismantled key protections against racial discrimination in voting, and they did it under the guise of a “colorblind Constitution.” The Times Opinion columnist Jamelle Bouie argues that in doing so, the court is resurrecting a form of judicial blindness that “gave us Jim Crow and so much worse.”

item.title: Anna Paulina Luna Wants Everything Disclosed
item.description: Anna Paulina Luna on Epstein, J.F.K. and U.F.O.s.

item.title: China Is ‘a Loss’
item.description: Trump’s China policy has “completely failed,” the political scientist Ian Bremmer argues on “The Ezra Klein Show,” and explains why America’s petrostate status isn’t enough to ensure its global dominance.

item.title: Children With Autism Lose When the Focus Is on Profits
item.description: Readers respond to an investigation into autism clinics accused of overcharging for a therapeutic method some say is harmful to children. Also: Weight-loss drugs for children.

item.title: Trump Is Finally Facing Reality on A.I.
item.description: A.I. will upend cybersecurity as we know it. The president’s new executive order is a good first step in creating a safer tech ecosystem.

item.title: ‘Hacks’ Is One of the Best Shows About Friendship I’ve Ever Seen
item.description: Two comedians walk into a … friendship.

item.title: What Elon Musk Really Wants From the SpaceX I.P.O.
item.description: This is the mercenary reason behind the SpaceX I.P.O.

item.title: The Fluffernutter Theory of Trump
item.description: Mindless optimism is the only antidote to rational despair.

item.title: The Boycott That Separated Me From My Neighbors
item.description: I’m not sure that I did the right thing by leaving the Park Slope Food Co-op.

item.title: S the Wolf
item.description: You spend your whole childhood trying to look different from your dad, only to wake up and see his forehead in the mirror.

item.title: Grow, Hair, Grow
item.description: You spend your whole childhood trying to look different from your dad, only to wake up and see his forehead in the mirror.

item.title: The Strait of Hormuz Is Blocked. The World Is Adjusting.
item.description: The longer we go without oil from the Persian Gulf, the less we’ll need it.

item.title: Ebola Shows Why Humanitarian Aid Matters
item.description: The fecklessness of Washington leaders contrasts with the courage of doctors, nurses and aid workers in places like Congo.

item.title: Why No Kings Isn’t Working
item.description: A case for mourning the American dream.

item.title: Contentious Times at the Supreme Court
item.description: Readers discuss judicial power and possible term limits. Also: Climate corruption; anxiety in children.

item.title: Stop Location-Tracking Your Friends and Lovers
item.description: Your friends don’t need to know where you are 24/7.

item.title: California Got the Candidates It Deserves
item.description: Why didn’t a great state get to choose among some great candidates? Blame the voters.

item.title: Spike Lee: Wemby Is Going Down!
item.description: The Knicks are New York’s soul, and they are bringing us together again in the championship series against San Antonio.

item.title: Feeling Nihilistic? You’re Not Alone.
item.description: A case for mourning the American dream.

item.title: Graham Platner, Ken Paxton and the Amoral Center
item.description: Why a morally divided country might find sleazy candidates reassuring.

item.title: The Gating of the American Dream
item.description: Social mobility in America is fundamentally broken, not because we have hollowed out the middle class but because we’ve hollowed out places, argues the political scientist Ian Bremmer on “The Ezra Klein Show.”

item.title: Tucker Carlson’s Big Bet
item.description: Is the Republican Party fracturing? The Opinion columnist Ezra Klein and the Republican strategist Liam Donovan dissect the growing divide between traditional conservatives and right-wing media figures like Tucker Carlson.
